title: 2 Speciedaler "Breddaler" - Christian IV

obverse.description: Standing figure of King Christian IV in full plate armor and without crown. King is holding a command staff in right hand and his figure is partially within a beaded circle with legend outside.
obverse.lettering: CHRISTIANVS • IIII • D: G: DANIÆ: NORV: VAND: GOTO: Q: REX •
obverse.lettering_translation: Christian IV King of Denmark, Norway of the Wends and the Goths

reverse.description: King on a roaring horse right, inside a circle with his motto along the inner rim. King is in full armor and wears an helm with long plumes; as the obverse he holds a command staff in right hand. Around there are the 14 provincial coats of arms outside the circle surrounding the rider, with King's Crown in upper position and the Pendant of the Order of the Elephant in the lower.
reverse.lettering:
REGNA: - FIRMAT - PIE - T - AS *
I S

comments:
Of this coin 2 version for obverse and two for reverse are known to exists:
Obverse 1: 14 rings in armor on hips and 6 rosette on shoulder plate
Obverse 2: 7 rings in armor on hips and 9 rosette on shoulder plate

Front 1: Isaac Henniges engraver (as described). Motto divided as: REGNA: - FIRMAT - PIE - T - AS. Initials I-S divided at sides of Elephant
Front 2: HB (unknown) engraver. Motto divided as: REGNA: - FIRM - AT - PIETAS with horse rider only partially within circle. Initials HB below horse back hooves.

Another example from Fritz Rudolf Künker GmbH & Co. KG, Auction 291, Lot 4067
